patch.value: GH-567: macOS+Windows nightly red — extension-install copy self-nesting check misses symlinked/short-name temp paths (realpath fallback asymmetry)

copyExtensionDirectoryWithoutSelfNesting (src/sdk/extension.ts ~L666-693, promoted in PR#565) realpaths the SOURCE but canonicalizes the DESTINATION via realpath(dirname(dest)).catch(() => destinationParent) — when the destination parent does not exist yet, the fallback keeps the NON-canonical path. On macOS os.tmpdir() is a symlink (/var/folders -> /private/var/folders) and on Windows mkdtemp can return 8.3 short names (RUNNER~1), so canonicalSource and canonicalDestination land in different path namespaces, isPathWithinDirectory misses the nesting, the plain-copy branch runs, and Node fs.cp fails with EINVAL 'cannot copy ... to a subdirectory of self'. Ubuntu /tmp is not a symlink -> green, which is why PR CI cannot catch it. First red: Nightly 2026-07-15 (run 29393793337) — macOS/Node24 leg is ENTIRELY this defect (1 fail/5719); windows/Node24 leg now has it as failure 7 of 7 (other 6 = pm-b24b + pm-u5zr). Class precedent pm-dprb/pm-gf6f (tmpdir canonicalization) but this is a PRODUCT defect, not spec-side: real installs on macOS/Windows hit EINVAL whenever the destination parent is not yet created. Fix direction: canonicalize the deepest EXISTING ancestor of the destination parent and re-append the remaining segments (or realpath both sides through a shared existing root) so both comparands are in the same namespace; never fix spec-side-only (pm-gf6f precedent).

isPathWithinDirectory comparison uses consistently canonicalized source AND destination when the destination parent does not exist, on symlinked (macOS) and short-name (Windows) temp paths; extension-copy-safety.spec.ts passes on all 5 nightly legs; regression coverage pins the nonexistent-destination-parent + symlinked-tmpdir case
